mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate signals easier discounting and lower financing stress, which lifts equity valuations and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. The effect is strongest when the rate is at or near the zero lower bound and fails if rate cuts coincide with worsening labor or output conditions that overwhelm valuation support.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not higher after FEDFUNDS declines versus flat or rising episodes, or if the sign flips in a rolling 3-year window with Rank IC below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over three months, the labor market is deteriorating fast enough to raise recession odds and compress expected earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal fails when unemployment rises from supply-side labor force re-entry without a concurrent slowdown in payroll growth.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes do not produce negative 20-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the signal remains negative when PAYEMS growth is strong and UNRATE is rising only mechanically.

mechanism_narrative: A broad monthly increase in RSAFS indicates resilient consumer spending and top-line revenue support for large-cap equities, which should translate into positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. The effect fails when higher sales are accompanied by surging inflation that erodes real demand or forces tighter monetary expectations.
failure_condition: Reject if positive changes in RSAFS do not precede above-baseline 20-day SPX returns, or if the relationship is absent in a rolling 3-year sample with Rank IC below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained drop in INDPRO signals weakening industrial activity and lower cyclical earnings expectations, which should push S&P 500 forward returns negative over the next month. The mechanism fails when production weakness is offset by disinflation and easier policy that re-rate equities upward.
failure_condition: Reject if monthly declines in INDPRO are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns more than 60% of the time, or if the effect disappears after controlling for FEDFUNDS.

mechanism_narrative: A CPIAUCSL inflation acceleration above 0.3% month over month while FEDFUNDS stays below 0.5% implies real rates are falling deeper into negative territory, which can support risky asset prices through liquidity and discount-rate channels. The signal is directional only during accommodation; it fails once policy is already tightening or inflation shocks trigger risk-off responses.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations do not show positive 5-day SPX returns versus baseline, or if the sign reverses once FEDFUNDS exceeds 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A stronger HOUST print indicates improved housing demand and a healthier interest-sensitive sector, which should lift risk appetite and produce positive S&P 500 returns over roughly one quarter. The mechanism fails when housing strength is purely inventory-driven or when higher starts coexist with falling payrolls and rising unemployment.
failure_condition: Reject if positive HOUST shocks do not align with positive 60-day SPX returns, or if the effect vanishes when PAYEMS weakens and UNRATE rises.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS growth turns negative and UNRATE rises together, the labor market is contracting in a way that directly reduces income expectations and raises default risk, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The signal fails if payroll losses are temporary but matched by rapid disinflation and imminent policy easing.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered instances do not generate negative 60-day SPX returns more than 55% of the time, or if the effect disappears after FEDFUNDS is falling sharply.

mechanism_narrative: A positive GDP growth surprise in GDPC1 relative to prior readings signals stronger aggregate demand and earnings breadth, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The relation fails when GDP improves only after inventory accumulation while sales, employment, and production remain weak.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth accelerates versus the prior quarter
failure_condition: Reject if GDP acceleration does not precede positive 60-day SPX returns, or if the effect is absent when RSAFS and INDPRO do not confirm the GDP improvement.
